text.title: Custom Guardrails Pipeline Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns specific to the hand-rolled pipeline.

antipattern.symptom: FastAPI handler blocks for ~600 ms on every request because all three checks run sequentially with blocking I/O.
antipattern.root-cause: PIIDetector + InjectionDetector are CPU-bound and OpenAI moderation is I/O-bound — but everything is awaited sequentially in a single coroutine.
antipattern.fix: Wrap CPU-bound detectors in `asyncio.to_thread` and run them in `asyncio.gather(...)` with the moderation call. Drops p99 from 600 ms to 200 ms.

antipattern.symptom: Email like `user.name+tag@subdomain.example.co.uk` slips past regex; SSN with no separator (`123456789`) slips past.
antipattern.root-cause: Naive regex tuned to common formats; missing `+tag`, multi-level TLD, separator-less SSN. Real PII formats are messier than canonical examples.
antipattern.fix: Adopt the Microsoft Presidio pattern set or maintain a versioned `PII_PATTERNS_V2` constant with edge cases (Unicode, +tag emails, no-separator SSN). Cover via golden-file tests in `guardrails-testing`.

antipattern.symptom: HallucinationDetector fires on every call regardless of context length; adds 500 ms to non-RAG traffic; doubles OpenAI bill.
antipattern.root-cause: Config flag is global on/off instead of conditional on `context` being non-empty.
antipattern.fix: Gate `enable_hallucination_detection` on `context is not None`. Add per-call escape hatch parameter to `process_output(skip_judge=True)`.

antipattern.symptom: To disable a single check in production, devs comment out code and redeploy; emergency hotfix needs a CI cycle.
antipattern.root-cause: Hardcoded component composition instead of `GuardrailConfig` dataclass with per-component bool flags.
antipattern.fix: Every component reads `self.config.enable_` at runtime; flags ship from env / feature-flag service. Disabling becomes a config change, not a deploy.
